text: Agile methodology is a popular approach for managing projects, especially in software development. Teams work in short cycles called sprints, usually lasting two to four weeks. Each sprint focuses on delivering a small, usable part of the project. Daily standup meetings help the team stay aligned; members quickly share what they did yesterday, what they plan to do today, and any obstacles. Before starting, the team prioritises tasks from a backlog, which is a list of features or requirements. These are often written as user stories, which describe a feature from the end user's perspective. This iterative development allows teams to adapt quickly to changes and improve the product over time.
